description: This diagram illustrates the geometric configuration of a satellite remote sensing system observing the Earth. A satellite, depicted as a blue rectangle with dimensions L (length) and D (width), travels along an `Orbit` (dotted red line) with `Satellite velocity` (red arrow). The satellite is positioned at an altitude `H` above the Earth's surface. Its sensor transmits a signal along the `Line Of Sight (range)`. The `Off-nadir angle (θ)` is defined as the angle between the vertical line from the satellite to the nadir and the `Line Of Sight (range)`. The beam illuminates the Earth's surface over an angular spread `α`, creating a ground coverage area known as the `Swath`. The `ground range` is indicated by a yellow arrow, representing the dimension perpendicular to the satellite's ground track. The `azimuth` direction is marked by a red arrow, aligned with the satellite's direction of motion. The `Antenna footprint in azimuth direction (β_a x R)` is shown on the ground, indicating the extent of the illuminated area along the azimuth.
